reactPageTitle: this page demonstrates various react components
reactNote: NB that recompiling the messages doesn't trigger a rebuild of the cached SSR component so it's easy to get hydration errors, not sure how to fix this yet apart from restarting the dev server but it does not affect production
staticReactText: This is text from a static react component, it is not client-side rendered
hydratedReactText: This text is from a react component that was SSR rendered then hydrated
hydrationRichText: This is a component with a custom rendered child component {#a href=|/|}home{/a}
clientReactText: This text is from a react component that only loaded client side
reactHydrationPageTitle: This page shows a few ways to circumvent the hydration issue
reactHydrationPassedProps: This passes children as props from the astro page, it can still run client effects when you click the button
langPropText: this element renders its own language, but it has had the lang prop passed to it from the Astro component, so it knows imperatively what language to build inside the astro island
pokemonFetchIntro: this page fetches (and route caches) pokemon from {#a href=|https://pokeapi.co|}https://pokeapi.co{/a}
pokemonFetchMarkupNote: N.B, the above paragraph with a link is rendered using `<ParaglideMessage/>`: a <Trans/> like react component, because React render prop JSX cannot be rendered inline in astro files, we have to import it from a seperate tsx file. Better off to wrap the whole thing in a static react component.
pokemonDynamicRoutingNote: The subsequent pages use dynamic routing to fetch pokemon data on-demand (cached and uncached version)
